title: aws-sm: Zeroize wrapper on binary put misleads about protection scope
description: Trigger: aws-sm put binary path. Code wraps bytes.to_vec() in Zeroizing, but Blob::new() copies bytes into AWS SDK-owned heap not under Zeroizing control. The comment implies the wrapper provides real protection; it only zeroes the local staging copy. Secret bytes live on in the Blob buffer and HTTP request body without zeroing. Fix: remove misleading Zeroizing wrapper and comment, or add honest comment that this only protects the local staging copy.
